steps.title: Find the antecedent
steps.content: The dependent clause's subject is "a given industry" — singular. The pronoun that opens the main clause must refer back to that singular noun. ("Water and electricity" are examples set off by dashes, not the grammatical subject.)

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content: A singular antecedent requires a singular pronoun and a singular verb. Only "it... consists" satisfies both.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — these often consist: plural pronoun and plural verb — violates pronoun-antecedent agreement with singular "industry." ✗
  • Choice B — they often consist: same problem — plural pronoun for a singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — it often consists: singular pronoun and singular verb that match "a given industry." ✓
  • Choice D — this often consists: "this" as a standalone pronoun is vague/incorrect here — a personal pronoun "it" is needed to refer to the specific antecedent "industry." ✗
That confirms C is correct.
